Resolution: It can be done by making a table report of final value after the compensation cycle and this report needs to be hosted on SFTP server and from there the value will be pulled with the help of integration on the job info field.

Following is the detailed reproduction of issue as well as the solution.

Employee’s Current Sub Role Band:

Change of Sub Role Band during ongoing compensation cycle. Screenshot of the final worksheet:

Now, we have to make a table report of the value (Final Pseudo Role Band) that need to be pushed from compensation to job info.

Steps to be followed for making a report:

Go to Report Centre. Click on New > Table Report > Single Domain > Compensation Planning.

Following is the screenshot of the report. Here for a user both the current and final values have been taken.

Steps For Scheduling a Report:

  • Click on three dots in the right side of the corresponding report.

  • Click on New Schedule. We get four sections to fill:
  • Schedule Definition
  • Filter
  • Destination
  • Schedule Occurrence

  • Give the name of the schedule. Select the file format. Here it is selected as csv. Choose the notification settings if required.
  • Click on step 2. There is nothing to choose at this step. Click on Step 3.
  • Here we give details about the SFTP server where this report file is going to be hosted.

 

  • Select FTP as the method. Then click on the checkbox saying SF hosted SFTP server and provide the credentials. Now provide the file path that is in which folder of SFTP we want to push the report.
  • Here the folder is /incoming. Also provide the file name containing the format as selected during Schedule definition. If we want to include the date format in our file name then select from the format as provided in the dropdown. Though it is not mandatory. After that click on Step 4.

  • Select the frequency of our schedule. Here it is selected as once as the final value is captured after the completion of compensation cycle. Then we need to select the date and time of our first occurrence.

  • After the successful completion of scheduling, the report will appear in the destination folder of SFTP server:

  • Next step is to create an integration to pull this report. Following are the steps:

Search Integration Center > Click on Create > Click on More Integration Types > Choose Integration Type

 

 

Select the options as below:

  • Trigger Type: Scheduled
  • Source Type: SFTP as our file is on SFTP
  • Destination Type: SuccessFactors (Value needs to be reflected on jobInfo portlet)
  • Format: CSV

Click on Create. Then search for job information in the search bar as we need to map the fields on the job information with our report fields. Next click on select.

 

  • In the options setting, select the integration name and write the description if needed. Then click on Next and move to the configure fields section.
  • Here we need to first upload a sample report file in the Sample Data View.

  • Click on Field Mapping View. Apart from mapping our destination field, we need to map certain mandatory fields (User Id, Start Date and Sequence Number).

  • We have to drag and drop User ID from source settings on to the User ID in destination fields to map them.
  • Similarly Final Pseudo Role Band is mapped.
  • Next, click on the Field Detail View and select the default value of sequence number.

  • Now select the start date of the integration. Here it is selected as Today. Else we can set calculation when we want the integration to start.

  • Select “Today” from the dropdown in front of the field value. Click ok.

  • After all the fields are mapped click on Next and configure the Source Settings.

  • File Server Settings to be configured as follows:
  • Enter the SFTP host address.
  • 22 is the default SFTP port.
  • In the authentication type select Basic Authentication.
  • Enter the SFTP credentials.
  • File Name and Folder Settings to be configured as follows:
  • Enter the name of the file (Hosted on SFTP server through Reporting Center) without file format.
  • Date suffix format to be selected if the file name contains the date.
  • Select the file extension as selected while scheduling the report.
  • Select the source folder of the SFTP.
  • Click on Next and set the schedule of the integration.

 

  • Email id can be mentioned if we want a notification for failed integrations. Then click on Next.

  • We need to click on Save once all the settings are configured. Then we can click on Run Now if we want to run the integrations or we can click on Set Schedule if we want the integration to run on our predefined scheduled date and time.
  • After that we can check the status of our integration from Last run time or by following the below steps:
  • Search Integration Center > Monitor Integration > Select the integration to see the details or errors if any.
  • Once the integration runs successfully, we need to check the value of our required field (Sub Role Band) in jobinfo.
  • Attached is the screenshot of jobinfo history notifying the change in value of Sub Role Band.

 

Regards,

Farhan Danish

entry.title: SuccessFactors Saudi Arabia End Of Service Calculator
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Introduction:

The end-of-service gratuity is considered one of the worker’s rights owed to the employer in the event of the termination of the work contract, and the Saudi legislator obliged the employer to pay it to the worker at the end of the work contract, whether the work contract was of a fixed-term or indefinite period. Therefore, with the upcoming solution I have made it easy for you to calculate the end-of-service benefit, the due date of the benefit, and how to calculate it according to the rules of the Saudi labor system according to the data already stored in the SuccessFactors Employee Central.

The Requirement:

To avoid calculating the EOS out side the system and do it directedly from SuccessFactors as same as the rest of processes and be able to document generation automatically from the system, we need to build a custom MDF (EOS Calculator) to set the required Workflow and generate the EOS documents and avoid any manual entry in ECP system.

The Challenge:

We need a complicated solution to calculate the End Of Service as per the KSA rule and regulations.

The EOS Calculations:                                                                                                         

EOS Basics: 

EOS (For 5 years of service and less) = 0.5 of One last month salary for each year = (0.5 X Last Month Salary X Service years)

EOS (For more than 5 years of service period) = (0.5 of One last monthly salary for 5 years) + (after 5 years will be One complete monthly salary for each year only the years after the 5 years) = (0.5 X One Monthly Salary X 5) + (Last Monthly Salary X remain service years)

Total Monthly Salary = Basic + Housing Allowance+ Trans+ Other Recurring Allowance

Payment Frequency (Number of days in Year) = 365                                                                                                                                                

Calculation - Example: 

Nov 2024 Period                                                                      

Actual Date of hire on 01.05.2017             

Terminated on 30.11.2024                                                    

No of Days 01.05.2017 to 30.11.2024 => 2770                                                            

Gratuity Basis -> Basic + Housing Allowance + Transportation + Other Recurring => 15000 + 3000 + 2000 + 2000 = 22000     

 No of Service year => 2770 / 365 => 6.219  

EOS for 5 years = 0.5 X 22000 X 5 = 55000                                               

EOS for 1.219 year = 1.219 X 22000 = 26818

EOS for 6.219 years = 55000 + 26818 = 81818                                        

The Cases:  

In Case of Resignation:

No. Of Years

Calculation

 Deserved EOS

From ZERO to 2 Years

ZERO End of Service

 0

From 2 Years TO 5 Years

1/3 End of Service Amount

81818 * 1/3 = 27272.666

From 5 Years TO 10 Years

2/3 End of Service Amount

81818 * 2/3 = 54545.333

From 10 Years and Above

Full End of Service Amount

81818

In Case of Termination:

Condition

Calculation

End of Employment Reason " Mutual Agreement 74"

Full End Of Service Amount + WT 6044 " Compensation Contract"

End of Employment Reason "Company no Reason 77"

Full End Of Service Amount + Last Basic Salary * WT ZEOS

End of Employments Reason "Labor law 80"

ZERO End Of Service & Only Leave Balance Encashment

Approach:

We will use 2 composite associated MDFs the parent MDF will do the basic calculations then the child MDF will do the rest of calculation and totals of dues, deductions and Net. 

The Solution:

The 2 composite associated MDFs as following:

  1. Finalization of Employee's Account ... The Parent MDF
  2. DUES Calculator ... The Child MDF

Let’s Start with First One the parent object: Finalization of Employee's Account

The custom fields:

  • End of Service Type                                                  
  • Join Date                                                      
  • Service Period  
  • Monthly Earning                                         
  • EOS Indemnity                                             

 The Associations:

Name: Total Dues

Multiplicity: One to One

Destination Object: DUES Calculator

Type: Composite                                                       

The Rules assigned:

1. In the effective start date field

  • EOS Hire Date Rule

  • EOS Monthly earning Rule, Monthly earnings calculated in a custom filed created in the compensation section by dividing the annual pay component group /12

2. In the cust_EOS_Type field

  • Add Evaluate Rule in the cust_EOS_Type field to calculate the service period and show up automatically once you chose the EOS Type as following:

3. In the cust_Service_Period filed

  • Add Evaluate rule to cust_Service_Period filed to calculate the EOS Indemnity as we explained earlier as following:   

4. In cust_EOS field

  • Add Evaluate Rule in cust_EOS to Calculate the Deserved EOS according to the type of termination as follow:

The second MDF the child object: DUES Calculator

The custom fields:

  • End OF Service Deserved
  • Annual Leave
  • Over Time
  • Bonus
  • Additional Amount from the Company
  • Notice Period Salary
  • Compensation Salary as Per Article 77
  • Personal Loan
  • Car Insurance
  • Other Deductions
  • Other Deduction Description
  • TOTAL DUES
  • TOTAL DEDUCTIONS
  • NET TO BE PAID / RECOVERED                               

The Rules assigned:

1. Add a Save Rule: To Calculate the total dues, total deductions and Net

2. In the EOS Deserved Field:

  • Add Evaluate Rule to Set amount with zero to make all custom added fields equal zero by default

 The EOS Calculator on the employee profile:

The end Result is an EOS calculator on the employee profile

Document Generation:

You Can build the document generation form to match the customer theme for example see the below:

Hope this help you in your KSA system configurations 

Thank you 

Ahmed Aranda

entry.title: Document Fails to Route to New Manager: Fixing Form Stuck Issues in the PMGM
entry.content:
Problem statement:

Frequent organizational changes and complex third-party integrations can result in inconsistent manager assignments, particularly for Employee Profile (EP) employees. While Employee Central (EC) handles automatic document transfers during manager changes, EP employees often face issues when conditions for automatic transfers aren’t met. This results in incorrect manager assignments, forms not routing properly, forms getting stuck with the former manager, former managers retaining access, and new managers being unable to evaluate performance.

This Excel-based workaround helps identify and fix incorrect manager assignments, allowing quick rerouting of forms to the correct manager. While SAP recommends "rocking" forms, SuccessFactors lacks a built-in solution for detecting false managers, especially during large organizational changes. The process is simple for anyone with basic Excel skills and can be enhanced with macros or automation tools, providing a practical solution for PMGM consultants during frequent restructuring.

The screenshot is from the KBA that provides details on 'Forms Not Transferring to New Manager' or 'Failed to Route to Manager.

Workaround:

Step 1: Create and Download Predefined Report from SuccessFactors Reporting Module

  • Generate the predefined report within the SuccessFactors Reporting Module and download it for further analysis
  • Navigate to the SuccessFactors Reporting module.

  • Create a Cross-Domain Table Report: Create a cross-domain table report using the Performance Management and Employee Profile (EP) domains. The EP domain identifies new manager/Matrix Manager changes, while the Performance Management domain shows the old manager to whom the form is stuck.

  • In the "People" tab, choose "Other Filters."

  • Select necessary columns: Choose the required columns for the workaround. The essential columns are listed below, but additional columns can be included based on specific requirements.

  • Apply filters to narrow down results: Under the 'Filters' tab, apply the following filters:
    • Set the form status to "Modify Stage."
    • Filter for "Active" forms only.

Additional filters can be applied as needed. Refer to this link for guidance on working with table reports.

Automation idea: This report can be automated by scheduling it to run at the desired frequency. The file can then be placed in the SFTP folder. For instructions on how to schedule a report, please refer to this link.

Step 2: Apply Excel formulas and filtering – This can be automated with Macros

a. Compare the current form owner with the manager/matrix manager using True/False formulas in Excel.

Collaborative review process

All steps here are based on the collaborative approach during the modify step. The collaborative review process (C Step) allows multiple roles to participate equally in the review at the same time.

Filter Forms based on Current Owner

The first step is to filter forms based on the current owner during the collaborative step. Forms can be accessed and edited by the employee, their manager, and their matrix manager. To identify and exclude false managers and matrix managers, we will use the following Excel formulas.

  • Open the Excel file downloaded from the report center.
  • Navigate to the Last Populated Cell.
  • Add the Following Columns
    • Add the "Employee Check" column.
    • Add the "Manager Check" column.
    • Add the "Matrix Manager Check" column.

  • Formula for "Employee Check":
    =IF(J2=E2, J2, False)
    • J2: User ID of the employee.
    • E2: User ID of the current owner of the form.
    • If the value in J2 (Employee User ID) matches E2 (Current Owner User ID), the formula will display the value in J2. Otherwise, it will display "False."

  • Formula for "Manager Check":
    =IF(O2=E2, O2, False)
    • O2: User ID of the employee’s manager.
    • E2: User ID of the current owner of the form.
    • If the value in O2 (Manager User ID) matches E2 (Current Owner User ID), the formula will display the value in O2. Otherwise, it will display "False."

  • Formula for "Matrix Manager Check":
    =IF(T2=E2, T2, False)
    • T2: User ID of the employee’s matrix manager.
    • E2: User ID of the current owner of the form.
    • If the value in T2 (Matrix Manager User ID) matches E2 (Current Owner User ID), the formula will display the value in T2. Otherwise, it will display "False."

  • Drag the formulas to all rows in the report

Points to Note:

  • The cell references in the formulas can be adjusted based on the specific Excel report generated.
  • The goal is to compare the current owner of the form with the Employee Profile column details. Since it's a collaborative step, the forms are accessible by all three roles at the same time. These forms will still reflect the old manager/matrix manager details. By using the Employee Profile columns, we can determine the current manager and matrix manager changes.

Based on this comparison, the owners of the form can be confirmed as follows:

    • If AC2 (Employee check cell) contains a user ID, the employee is the owner of the form for that step.
    • Similarly, the owner of the form will be identified using the user IDs in AD2 (Manager check cell) and AE2 (Matrix Manager check cell).
    • If any of these cells show '0', it means the current owner user ID is empty, indicating that no manager or matrix manager is assigned to that employee.

Apply Filters: The "Employee Check" column will always be set to "False," allowing us to identify the population of Managers and Matrix Managers. However, the "Manager Check" and "Matrix Manager Check" columns will still contain data for both True Managers and Matrix Managers, as well as False Managers and Matrix Managers.

  • First, filter the "False" values in the Employee Check, Manager Check, and Matrix Manager Check columns.
  • Filter for active or blank status of Manager
  • This will give us the following:
    1. A population of False Managers and False Matrix Managers.
    2. A False Manager in the Manager Check column could also be a Matrix Manager.
    3. A False Matrix Manager in the Matrix Manager Check column could also be a Manager.

  • Copy Document IDs for the False Population: Copy the Document IDs of the population where all three columns (Employee Check, Manager Check, and Matrix Manager Check) are set to "False" into a separate sheet named "All False".
  • Remove duplicates from the list of Doc Ids

Above steps are essential for routing the forms for huge population to update the new manager/matrix manager details.

b. Identifying Cases with a Single Performance Form Owner and Reassigned Manager

The screenshot below shows that the form owner is only the employee, with the other two fields left blank. After assigning a new manager, the form needs to be "rocked" to reflect the change. To identify document IDs with a single owner, we will use Pivot Tables.

  • Clear Filters in the Main Report
    • Clear any existing filters applied in the main report.
    • Click on the "Select All" button (located at the intersection of row numbers and column letters in the top-left corner of the worksheet).

  • Insert a Pivot Table
    • With all the data selected, insert a Pivot Table into a new sheet. 

Steps in Pivot Table for Manager:

  1. Set Up Pivot Table Fields:
    • Place "Document ID" into the Rows field.
    • Place "Current Owner User ID" into the Values field (set to Count of Current Owner User ID). This will count how many times each owner is assigned to a document.
    • Place "Manager Status" into the Filters field. This allows you to filter and view the forms based on whether the manager is active or inactive.
  2. Filter for Active Managers:
    • In the Pivot Table, set the Manager Status filter to Active. 
  3. Sort Results:
    • Sort the results in the Pivot Table in ascending order (from A to Z). 
  4. Identify and Copy Document IDs:
    • If the Count of Current Owner User ID is 1, copy the corresponding Document IDs into another sheet ‘Pivot Manager’ for further processing.
    • If the Count of Current Owner User ID is not equal to 1(<>1), ignore that entry.

By repeating these steps for the Matrix Manager column, you'll be able to identify forms associated with active-matrix managers and properly route them as needed.

Steps in Pivot Table for Matrix Manager:

  1. Set up Pivot Table Fields:
    • Place "Document ID" into the Rows field.
    • Place "Current Owner User ID" into the Values field (set to Count of Current Owner User ID). This will count how many times each owner is assigned to a document.
    • Place "Matrix Manager Status" into the Filters field. This will allow you to filter and view the forms based on whether the matrix manager is active or inactive.
  2. Filter for Active-Matrix Managers:
    • In the Pivot Table, set the Matrix Manager Status filter to Active. 
  3. Sort Results:
    • Sort the results in the Pivot Table in ascending order (from A to Z). 
  4. Identify and Copy Document IDs:
    • If the Count of Current Owner User ID is 1, copy the corresponding Document IDs into another sheet ‘Pivot matrix manager’ for further processing.
    • If the Count of Current Owner User ID is not equal to 1(<>1), ignore that entry.

Step 3: Final Document IDs uploaded in the Route Form tool

Prepare the final Document IDs:

After identifying the forms with incorrect managers or matrix managers, forms with one Owner gather the Document IDs from the "All False," "Pivot Manager," and "Pivot Matrix Manager" sheets that need routing. These are the forms that require manager reassignment (Please check how to mass route forms).

Upload Document IDs to Route Form Tool:

  • Log in to the SuccessFactors Admin Centre
  • Use the "Route Form" tool to upload the final Document IDs that were identified in the previous steps. 
  • Upload the CSV file containing the document IDs.

Step 4: Rock the form for the changes to reflect

  1. Route the Forms:
    • After uploading the Document IDs into the Route Form tool, initiate the routing process to the correct manager or matrix manager.
  2. Final check:
    • Double-check that all forms have been correctly routed and updated to reflect the new manager and matrix manager assignments.

Additional checks and analysis part:

To identify the inactive managers/matrix managers

Previously, I have only focused on the active or blank status of the manager/matrix manager. For managers with an inactive status, I will identify the appropriate active manager, assign them, and then repeat steps 3 and 4.

    • Clear any existing filters applied in the main report.
    • Filter “Inactive User” in „Manager status “column.
    • Identify the correct manager and route the forms as above(step 3 and 4).
    • Repeat the above steps by filtering “Inactive User” in „Matrix Manager status “column.

Further Analysis in identifying accurately the Wrong Manager/Wrong Matrix Manager:

Above steps 1 through 4 fetches me the document Ids to rock the forms. The following extra steps help determine if an employee has the wrong manager or matrix manager:

Identifying the Wrong Manager:

  • After creating a new sheet with the Document IDs, clear all filters.
  • Reapply filters:
      1. Set the "Employee Check" column to False.
      2. Set the "Manager Check" column to False.
      3. For the "Matrix Manager Check" column, apply a filter to show only rows with numbers (exclude "False" and "0"). This will display the correct Matrix Manager assigned to the form owner.

From these results, you can identify employees assigned with wrong manager, pinpointing where the form is stuck. Copy this population into a separate sheet named "False Manager" for further analysis.

Identifying the Wrong Matrix Manager:

Repeat the previous steps, but change the filter in the "Manager Check" column:

  • After creating a new sheet with the Document IDs, clear all filters.
  • Reapply filters:
      1. Set the "Employee Check" column to False.
      2. In the "Manager Check" column, apply a filter to show only rows with numbers. This will display the correct Manager assigned to the form owner.
      3. Set the "Matrix Manager Check" column to False.

From these results, you can identify employees assigned with wrong matrix manager, showing where the form is stuck. Copy this population into a separate sheet named "False Matrix Manager" for further analysis. 

These steps help narrow down and pinpoint the exact issue with the Manager and Matrix Manager assignment such as if the manager is blank, or incorrect, etc.

The above screenshot can be interpreted as follows: The new manager's change is not reflected in the form as the "current owner user ID" column is blank.

Conclusion:

This proactive approach improved form maintenance by ensuring correct form ownership, preventing missing steps in route maps, and avoiding the creation of unnecessary steps that could cause confusion and permission issues.

Our customer runs this report with Macros and finds it extremely helpful. It saves a significant amount of time, especially when handling multiple requests related to forms stuck due to manager assignments and when needing to route large populations. I hope this workaround will be beneficial for others facing similar challenges.

I would love to hear about your experiences and insights on how you’ve addressed such issues. If you have any suggestions for enhancing or improving this workaround, please feel free to share them. If you have any questions or comments, don’t hesitate to add them below.

Thank you for taking the time to read through this blog! 🙂

entry.title: Grievance Tracking Extension Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM
entry.content:
1) Purpose of the Blog

The purpose of this blog is to highlight Tech Envo's Grievance Tracking Solution, designed to foster a transparent and compliant workplace by enabling employees to easily report concerns. Built with workplace compliance in mind, this solution streamlines grievance management, ensuring accountability, quick resolution, and improved organizational trust. By leveraging a user-friendly interface and robust tracking capabilities, the solution enhances transparency, reduces manual effort, and promotes a positive work environment. This blog aims to showcase how the solution addresses key challenges in grievance handling while empowering organizations to maintain a fair and compliant workplace.


 2) Architecture Design of the Grievance Tracking SAP BTP Extension Solution

 

The architecture of the Grievance Tracking Extension Solution is designed for efficiency, security, and seamless integration with SAP environments. The user interface, built with SAP UI5 Fiori Elements, provides a responsive and intuitive experience, allowing employees and HR administrators to easily navigate the grievance management system.

At its core, the backend leverages the SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model and Node.js, ensuring robust data processing, workflow automation, and secure integration with SAP SuccessFactors through an SAP BTP destination. This architecture enables organizations to deploy the solution as a standalone application within SAP Build Work Zone or embed it as a portlet within SAP SuccessFactors, offering flexibility and scalability in grievance management.

Hosted on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), the solution ensures data security, compliance, and uninterrupted access, empowering organizations to streamline grievance tracking, enhance transparency, and promote a fair workplace environment.

3)  What is the Grievance Tracking Extension Solution?


 Our powerful SaaS- based Grievance Tracking Extension Solution empowers HR administrators to efficiently manage and resolve employee concerns with ease. Designed to handle cases related to harassment, discipline, and general grievances, this solution streamlines the entire process—from submission to resolution. With an automated approval workflow, grievances are systematically reviewed by management and escalated to arbitration boards when necessary, ensuring a fair, transparent, and consistent resolution process. By fostering accountability and reinforcing your organization’s commitment to employee well-being, this solution helps create a more supportive and compliant workplace.

 

4)   How TechEnvo’s Grievance Tracking Extension Solution Benefits Organizations

  1. Effortless & Transparent Grievance Reporting
    Empower employees with a simple, intuitive grievance submission process—just a few clicks, and their concerns are officially recorded. Our automated workflows ensure swift processing, guaranteeing timely resolutions while maintaining complete transparency throughout the journey.


     

    2.Comprehensive Grievance Lifecycle Management
    From initiation to resolution, every grievance is meticulously documented within the system. Even after closure, all cases remain securely stored for easy retrieval, ensuring compliance, audit readiness, and accountability in every step.

     

    3. Flexible Access for Seamless Integration
     Tailored to fit your organization’s unique needs:
    • Standalone Application – Available within SAP Build Work Zone on SAP BTP for a seamless and adaptable experience.
    • SAP SuccessFactors Integration – Easily embedded as a portlet for organizations already leveraging SAP SuccessFactors, ensuring a smooth and uninterrupted workflow.


       4. Built-in Document Management for Streamlined Operations
           Effortlessly upload, store, and retrieve grievance-related documents within the application—no need    for external document management tools. Everything you need, all in one place.

 With our Grievance Tracking Extension Solution, transform the way your organization handles grievances—promoting fairness, transparency, and efficiency at every step.


5) Why Choose the Grievance Tracking Solution?

A workplace that listens is a workplace that thrives. Addressing employee grievances effectively is key to fostering a positive, productive, and compliant work environment.

 
  • End-to-End Grievance Management – Seamlessly create, track, and resolve grievance cases while involving union representatives for fair and structured dispute resolution. All related documents can be securely uploaded, ensuring a well-documented and transparent process.
  • Built on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) – Leverage the power of SAP BTP for a seamless, scalable, and high-performance solution that integrates effortlessly with existing SAP systems.
  • Seamless SAP SuccessFactors Integration – Organizations using SAP SuccessFactors can embed this solution directly into their workflows, providing a consistent and intuitive user experience.
  • Compliance & Workplace Excellence – Stay ahead of workplace regulations while cultivating an environment where employees feel heard, valued, and empowered to contribute their best.

     

    With TechEnvo’s Grievance Tracking Solution, organizations can proactively manage grievances, drive transparency, and build a culture of trust and accountability.

     

    6) Get Started with the Grievance Tracking Extension Solution

    The Grievance Tracking Extension Solution is now available to help organizations create a transparent, efficient, and structured approach to managing employee grievances. Powered by SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), this solution simplifies grievance handling while ensuring compliance and accountability.

    With its seamless integration into SAP SuccessFactors or as a standalone application within SAP Build Work Zone, HR administrators can efficiently track, document, and resolve employee concerns in a secure and automated environment.

    🔹Enhance workplace transparency
    🔹Ensure compliance with labor regulations
    🔹Streamline grievance resolution with structured workflows

    Ready to transform your grievance management process? Contact us today to see how this solution can empower your workforce and strengthen your organization’s commitment to employee well-being!

entry.title: Automate Username Updates to Previous Employment Records
entry.content:
AD Writeback in Rehire With New Employment

Good day

Another interesting one for a bit of architecting.  In many hybrid implementations, rehire with new employment is quite a usual scenario.  With SSO / IAS / Authentication against Azure IdP (or whatever your particular scenario), you might have come across a scenario where the AD UPN / SamAccountName had been created on the now-terminated employment record’s username, and the new employment record’s username is the same as the new User ID.  So, today I will be looking at ways to be able to automate the process of updating the username for the previous employment, so the any automated processes that tries to write the same AD UPN/SamAccountName back to the new User ID, would succeed.

First, there are two scenarios in my particular example:  A permanent employee terminated in one Legal Entity to join another (typical of hybrid implementations), or when a contingent worker joins as a permanent employee.  The first can be achieved by leveraging the Intelligent Services Centre (ISC) event for terminations and an Integration Centre (IC) job.  Note, however, that when a Work Order ends, the Contingent becomes inactive, but a termination is not logged in ISC.

Permanent Employees

I have adapted my previous blog post logic that I previously wrote about updating the username for inactive employees here:  instead of scheduling a recurring job in Integration Centre (IC), I use the ISC Termination Event to trigger the update.  Also, instead of pre-pending the username with ‘i_’, I simply map the user ID to the Username – this will ensure that it always has a unique username without the need to worry about a second or third rehire with new employment.

In IC:

 

In ISC:

 

In this particular case, I chose to let the IC job update the username a day after the effective termination date, to prevent possible issues with logging on while still finishing off the last day of work.

Contingent Worker

As mentioned above, an ISC Termination event is not raised when a work order ends.  In this scenario, I have created an IC scheduled job, restricted to only update usernames for job info records where there is an ECWK event reason (which will be on the day that the contractor’s status will be inactive).

In IC:

The field mappings look exactly like those for permanent employees.  In addition, I have added the following filters:

 

 

In my particular scenario, I set up the schedule to trigger every two hours, 12 times a day to cover the 24-hour rolling period.  Depending on your own use case, you might decrease the frequency.

 

That’s it.  Now the username on the old user ID will be updated to the unique User ID, thus allowing and automated writeback processes to successfully write the username to the new User ID / Employment Record.

If this helps, give a kudo!  If not, well, feel free to reach out and I am happy to look at a different architecture that might work for you.

Regards

Eugene

entry.title: Data Migrations in SuccessFactors using MS Access as ETL
entry.content:
As I look back on the 5+ years on a SuccessFactors Employee Central (SF-EC) installation at this client I have worked, the Data Migration carries all the challenges of a Greenfield implementation if not more.A retail giant’s 200,000+ personnel related updates within a short time window, managing the middleware / integrations’ downstream impacts and to anticipate existing possible data inaccuracies which would result in the new data in errors.

I will reflect on my learnings on using SF-EC adhoc reports and MS Access Databases to run ETL processes bringing in consistency, reliability, accuracy in importing quality data into SF-EC

Learning 1: Extracting complex position information from SF-EC

The standard import and export data feature exports the Position Information along with its historical data including inactive records. If you choose only active records, it will drop the latest time slice where it was made inactive and will give an incorrect picture.

The .csv export is so huge that it crosses 1 GB and the import into MS Access fails. The alternative approach I took was to split the .csv using an executable splitter app I had downloaded, then stitch them back from 3 Access DBs and reference this stitched DB for further analysis of current and future active records.

From my learnings and continuous improvements, subsequently I developed a canvas report to extract Position Information from SF-EC to extract the current and future records. This report had 105 fields including values and their titles. But once this was ready it was much easier to extract this information which I pretty much needed on a daily basis to bring in insights for the business teams working with us to understand SF-EC data.

The definition of current and future within the adhoc canvas data has to be carefully built in the filter criteria as shown in the below clipping here

 The joins within the tables get developed as below while noticing that you need to add filters to start and end dates for the other linked objects else there will be duplicate records found on your report

 

Now to follow this supply chain of data into MS Access, we run a macro developed which runs the import of this canvas report into an MS Access table from a pre-specified path on the local folder. This forms the data warehouse from which many other MS Access databases will extract this data and run other ETL operations as necessary.

It took a few weeks to stabilize this report but the initial investment paid off on a daily basis, so much so that the Rewards team leads would use my report as their standard starting point for their subsequent processes.

Learning 2: Position uplift in SF-EC, using MS Access end-to-end automations

In 2020, we started to implement the Position Uplift project which encompassed more associations around master data objects like Personnel Area / Subarea, Group, Brand, Ops Support, Region & Area etc. The matrix I built for my own reference captured the essence of the requirements on a master data level. This enables a much better data integrity at the position creations stage in SF-EC.

 

An interesting challenge in preparing the ETL automation process was the Personnel Area / SubArea (PA-PSA) master data. PA-PSA had an association to another field Brand Location in the sense that we needed to arrange multiple Brand Locations in a single row separated by “|” for each PA-PSA. The logic to arrive at Brand Location was also a reverse engineering exercise to see what existed as Brands and Location information in existing Position records and making a unique list from that reverse engineered data.

Eventually when you have vertical data needing to be transposed into horizontal, there is no easy way in MS Access so I reverted to a semi-automatic process of doing this transpose in Excel and then continuing the onward journey of automation in MS Access. This gives you extreme flexibility and you don’t need to struggle to find the best solution, rather develop a sustainable and prompt solution to keep going ahead further.

We ran 3 Trial Conversions with almost 15+ data objects which were interconnected as seen in the above matrix, by developing automations in ETL using SF-EC adhoc reports, Excel and MS Access, the latter being the engine of these operations. We then had to update the positions and the job information records based on these new associations, making sure that the reverse engineered master data fell in place for the positions and job information. This took us a year to finally go ahead with a smooth go-live with the Position Uplift.

Learning 3: Annual SGC % update in SF-EC Job Information

Legislative requirements of an annual SGC% (Super Guarantee Charge) mandates us to update 200,000 Job Information data in SF-EC annually around June. This ETL has been developed by me in MS Access where we have certain filter criteria applied for ignoring contractors & some special EBA arrangement employees. The challenge here has always been to manage these data imports in SF-EC in packet size by working in collaboration with several integration and downstream teams which take SF-EC data on a regular basis. These downstreams need to ensure there is no bottleneck in this annual one-time massive amounts of data flowing through. The older workflows which would then be approved later after these SGC% updates would also need to be managed since these workflows would replace the new SGC% with the older one.

Through my 25+ years of SAP projects journey, I have used several ETL products like SAP Data Services, Informatica, Syniti (used to be Cransoft back then), MS Access, the importance of understanding SQLs and their effectiveness in generating insights cannot be under estimated. The key is to ask the client which of these tools exist already and use them for cost effective data migrations.

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Dependent Management: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
1. Overview:
SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central allows organizations to manage employees' dependents efficiently. This enables HR to store and maintain dependents' details for benefits administration, tax calculations, etc.
2. HRIS Elements:
According to SAP KBA 2807618, in SuccessFactors Employee Central, dependents are managed through the Dependents Information Portlet, which includes:

   • A specific HRIS element: personRelationshipInfo
   • Reused fields from various HRIS elements (as listed in the table)

Limitations:

  • Limited customization for reused fields
  • No RBP support for reused HRIS elements

 Note 1: You can add custom fields under the personRelationshipInfo HRIS element, and they can be managed via RBP, newly added fields will be unchecked by default in RBP, so you’ll need to manually enable permissions for them as needed

 Note 2: If you need to customize fields from reused HRIS elements, DO NOT modify the original element, as changes will affect both Dependents and Employees. Since RBP is not supported, create a new Person Type under the base HRIS element instead.

Navigation Path: Manage business configuration→Select the base HRIS element → configure New Person Type.

Select the Person Type as Dependent then save.

Now A new HRIS element has been created for Dependents, allowing customization such as disabling fields, changing visibility, and sorting. However, some limitations remain as it is still linked to the base PersonalInfo element.
Limitations:
   • Field labels cannot be changed.
   • In correction mode, fields display only their identifiers.                                                                                     • Custom fields appear as String20, Date1, etc. So  Always refer to the main HRIS element to correctly identify fields before making changes.

We can repeat the same steps for the PersonInfo if any customizations are needed.

Note 3: According to KBA 2634641 & KBA 3235376, National ID and Home Address fields for dependents CANNOT be managed using RBP, even by creating a new Person Type.
Available Options:
   ✓ Hide the entire section from the Dependents Information Portlet
   ✓ Keep it as it is (no RBP control possible)
To Hide the Address section from dependents portlet:

Select the main HRIS homeAdress→Make correction →Disabled under Dependents Section = YES

To Hide the National ID section from dependents portlet:

Select the main HRIS NationalID→Make correction →Disabled under Dependents Section = YES

3. User Interface and RBP:
Access to the Dependents UI (portlet) will be Based on:
   • Users' ability to view or modify certain fields depends on their permissions.
   • Customizations previously done will influence what is displayed.
To grant permission to the users:
Access Manage Permission Roles→ Select the desired role and click Edit→ Skip Editing the basic info by clicking the ‘NEXT’ button→ In the Add Permission screen, search for Dependents, grant the necessary permissions, and save.

Example: Visa File Number is a custom newly added field under the personRelationshipInfo

Adding Information about Dependents (ESS):
   • The UI will show relevant fields based on the employee permissions.
   • Users can Add, Edit, and delete details for dependents.
   • Make sure to click the “Edit details” button at the bottom of the dependents screen and fill in all required fields to avoid warnings, errors, or information messages.

4. Bulk Data Management:

4. a. Importing Dependents details:

According to SAP KBA 2092840, We have to download 3 main uploading templates from Import Employee Data:
1. Biographical Information: To create the dependent as a new person in the system:

Navigation Path: Access Import Employee Data→ Select Download Template →Select
Biographical Information→ Select the desired fields From Available data fields Tab→ Click
Generate Template.

Repeat the same steps to download the remaining templates.

Give the code for the country of birth(for example TUN for Tunisia).(A list of some country codes will be added to the end of this article)

Fill in the DOB: MM/DD/YYYY

user ID: keep blank

Person ID External: This will be the unique identifier for the dependent and will be reused for
other uploading templates.
Note: Make sure to enter a unique ID that has never been used before for any other
person type, as this is the same template used to upload employee data.

2. Person Relationships: To create the link between the employee and his dependents:

Person ID External: Fill with the employee ID
Related Person Id External: Give the Dependent Unique ID created in the previous template.    Relationship: Enter the value according to your configured relationship picklist (refer to the Picklist Center). Do the same for any value that will be added to a picklist-type field.

Note: Customized fields created under personRelationshipInfo will appear in this Template.

3. Personal Information: Upload the dependents' personal data such as First name, Last name…:

For Home Airport and Salutation: Enter the value according to your configured picklist (refer to
Picklist Center). Do the same for any value that will be added to a picklist-type field.

 

Note: You can add additional Information for the dependents like NationalID info…
Data Uploading: 

Access Import Employee Data→ Select Import Data→ FIRST Select Biographical Information→ Select the Data import  File→ Validate then Import

Repeat the same steps to Import the remaining templates In the same order 1. biographical info 2.Relationship 3.personalInfo.

4. b. Exporting Dependents data:

Please refer to SAP Public KBA: 2806252 - How to Retrieve Dependents Related Information via Canvas Reports. It provides a detailed and clear guide on exporting dependent details.

However, please note that the Dependent Related Person ID extracted in the report is different from the Dependent Unique ID created during the import. To pull the exact ID used during the import, add the Dependent Person ID column under the Dependent Personal Info table (Step 3 in the second screenshot)

4. C. DELETE/ DELIMIT Dependents: 

To DELETE an employee's dependent record from the Dependents portlet in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central can be challenging, especially when dealing with large amounts of data.
Performing a Full Purge import requires reimporting all dependent data, and if you want to completely delete records by uploading an empty file, the system will not allow it.


As per SAP KBA 2411208, the Person Relationship operation does not support a DELETE function but instead uses the DELIMIT option. Here are the two methods to handle the deletion of dependent records:
  ✓ If Data is Available in the Import Template:
   • In Incremental import mode, add the DELIMIT operation to the Person Relationship record in the import template.
   • Ensure that the Effective Start Date for the operation matches the Effective Start Date of the dependent record.
   • When the DELIMIT operation is executed, it will effectively "close" the relationship record for that dependent and Remove it from the History. But Dependents data will remain in the system.
   • Further steps: You can Add the DELETE operation to the Personal Info, and the remaining used Import files accordingly

  ✓ If Data is not Available in the Import Template:
   • Step 1: Retrieve Person ID External or Dependent Person ID via Data Inspector or canvas Report (exported in ‘Exporting Dependents data’ Section)
   • Step 2: Export and fill Person Relationship template with Person ID External employee ID, Related Person ID External, Dependent Person ID, event date, relationship-type and DELIMIT in Operator column.
   • Step 3: Import fie in incremental mode and verify that dependent is no more shown in employee's profile.


For Additional details, You can Follow the Guidelines mentioned in SAP Public KBA 2274679 - How To Delete/Delimit Dependents Data via Import - Employee Central.


To DELIMIT a dependent relationship while keeping it as historical data, you should add the DELIMIT operator in the import template, and ensure the Effective Start Date is different from the record's start date. This will mark the dependent record as inactive without deleting it from the system, allowing it to remain in the system as historical data.
Example:
If the dependent's original Effective Start Date is 2023-01-01, and you want to delimit the record as of 2024-01-01, you would:
• Add the DELIMIT operation in the import template.
• Set the Effective Start Date for the DELIMIT operation to 2024-01-01, which is the new date when the relationship will be ended.


This approach ensures that the dependent's record is still stored as historical data but is no longer active as of the effective start date of the DELIMIT operation.

 

Country  Alpha-3 Code
Tunisia TUN
India IND
Germany DEU
United States USA
France FRA
United Kingdom GBR
Canada CAN
Australia AUS
Italy ITA
Spain ESP
Netherlands NLD
Belgium BEL
Switzerland CHE
Austria AUT
Sweden SWE
Norway NOR
Denmark DNK
Finland FIN
Japan JPN
China CHN
India IND
Brazil BRA
South Africa ZAF
Russia RUS
Mexico MEX
Saudi Arabia SAU
United Arab Emirates ARE

entry.title: Secure connection configuration in Employee Central and Employee Central Payroll in Grow with SAP
entry.content:
As we know that EC(Employee Central) and ECP (Employee Central Payroll) are interdependent  and they communicate with each other .Recently I got opportunity to  configuration connection between EC to ECP system on SAP on public cloud This blog post  describes step-by-step in how to configure connection with help of OAuth 2.0  which is the industry standard protocol for authorization.

The prerequisites:

1-Check, in which support package your service is available.

2-Parameter icm/HTTPS/verify_client is set to 1 (accept certificates) or 2 (require certificates).

3-Make sure that you’ve maintained the optional fields for System ID and API .

4-Activate SAP Gateway

5-Ensure that the Local Provider follows the format <System ID>_<Client>,

6-Check system alias for OData service id not then create it

Configuration step

1-Certificate generation

1-Login on EC system Go to Admin Center  Security Center and choose X509 Certificates

 

 

 

Choose Add to create a New X509 Certificate.

 

 

Please  create 2 certificate as per below naming format

<EC Instance Name>_<System ID>_<Client>_ADM

<EC Instance Name>_<System ID>_<Client>_ESS

Select SAP Cloud Root CA as Certification Authority (CA

 

 

 

Click on Generate and Save, to save your certificate key and download

 

 

 

2-Configuring OAuth Identity Provider

Login on ECP system and run t-code SAML2

 

 

It will open saml2 screen in new window click on trusted provider then Add   Manually

You need to add 2 trusted provider format should be as below-

<EC Instance Name>_ADM,

<EC Instance Name>_ESS

Choose next and Primary Signing Certificate and upload certificate which you have created in SC system and save it  you must perform activity for both provider which is mention above

 

Once both providers created then go to main screen and change it to

In the details section of your newly created Identity Provider, choose Add. In the Supported NameID Formats window, select Unspecified and choose OK.

In the details section of NameID Format "Unspecified", select Logon ID  or login alias as User ID Mapping Mode.If you are using SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication for Employee Central Payroll, then select Logon Alias as User ID Mapping Mode.

 

 

 

 

3-Creating Service Users

 

 

Go to transaction SU01 in ECP system and create following service users:

EC_ADM_OAUTH

 

 

 

 

 

EC_ESS_OAUTH

 

 

 

4-Registering OAuth Client

login on ECP system and rum t-code SOAUTH2 in OAuth 2.0 Administration  choose Create  create below 2

EC_ADM_OAUTH

EC_ESS_OAUTH

 In the Create OAuth 2.0 Client window, select a OAuth 2.0 Client ID, provide a Description and choose Next.

In the Client Authentication step, ensure SSL Client Certificate is checked and choose Next.

In the Resource Owner Authentication step, ensure Grant Type SAML 2.0 Bearer Active is checked. In the Trusted OAuth 2.0 IdP field, choose the identity provider you already created in the Configuring OAuth Identity Provider section and choose Next.

In the Scope Assignment step, add a OAuth 2.0 Scope ID, according to your client ID and choose Next:

Final screen will be as below-

 

 

5-Importing X.509 Certificates

Run strust t-code in ECP system and import previously create certificates from EC system in SSL server standard

 

 

 

 

 

6-Mapping X.509 Certificates with Table USREXTID

1 Run transaction SM30, open view VUSREXTID in edit mode.

2 Enter DN in the External ID type field.

3 Choose New Entries and import the certificate.

4 Provide following user names: EC_ESS_OAUTH

5 Set the Activated indicator to activate the client certificate logon for the user.

6 Save your entries and the assignment of External ID to Users will be displayed.

Repeat the same steps for user EC_ADM_OAUTH.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

7-Mapping X.509 Certificates with Table CERTRULE

1-Go to transaction CERTRULE.

2-Switch to edit mode and import your certificate in the Subject field.

3-Choose the Explicit Mapping button.

4- Select user EC_ESS_OAUTH and continue.

5-The new entries are displayed in the Explicit Mappings tab.

6-Save your entries.

Repeat the same steps for user EC_ADM_OAUTH.

 

 

 

 

 

Now you can check it at ECP system it will ask for EC system credential and authenticate from EC system

 

 

Reference-

ahttps://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_EMPLOYEE_CENTRAL_PAYROLL/185f14fbe60d4bbb8d7d5e4f8d89b24b/bb1f11be38134ae3aac6b3139297ffc5.html
